skill.name: git-pr-workflows-onboard
skill.description: Expert onboarding specialist for new team member integration with customized 30/60/90 day plans, technical setup guidance, and team culture immersion.
skill.summary: Creates comprehensive onboarding plans for new hires including pre-arrival prep, Day 1 orientation, technical setup, milestone tracking, and team integration activities.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 21 static analysis findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' detections reference markdown documentation and checklist examples, not actual shell execution. The 'cryptographic' and 'reconnaissance' patterns matched innocent text in onboarding documentation. This skill generates text-based onboarding plans with no code execution capabilities.

content.user_title: Create personalized onboarding plans for new hires
content.value_statement: New employee onboarding often lacks structure and consistency, leading to slow ramp-up and poor retention. This skill transforms onboarding into a systematic process with role-specific plans, measurable milestones, and proven integration strategies.
actual_capabilities: Generate customized onboarding plans based on role, seniority, and location
Create Day 1, Week 1, and 30/60/90 day milestone checklists
Provide technical environment setup guidance for developers
Design team integration activities and buddy system frameworks
Build feedback collection mechanisms and onboarding metrics tracking
Adapt plans for remote, hybrid, or on-site team structures
limitations: Cannot directly create accounts or provision system access
Generated plans require human validation for organization-specific requirements
Depends on accurate input data about role and team context
Does not replace manager involvement in onboarding process

use_cases.title: Software Engineer Onboarding
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive 30/60/90 day plan for new developer including development environment setup, first PR guidance, codebase immersion activities, and team integration milestones.

use_cases.title: Remote Employee Integration
use_cases.description: Generate remote-specific onboarding plan with virtual orientation sessions, async communication norms, timezone-friendly scheduling, and home office setup guidance.
use_cases.target_user: HR coordinators managing distributed team onboarding

use_cases.title: Senior Hire Accelerated Onboarding
use_cases.description: Build fast-track onboarding for senior or lead hires including leadership introductions, architecture deep-dives, stakeholder mapping, and early ownership opportunities.
use_cases.target_user: CTOs or VPs onboarding senior technical leaders

prompt_templates.title: Basic Onboarding Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an onboarding plan for a new Software Engineer starting next Monday. Role: mid-level backend developer. Team: 8 person platform team. Location: remote in EST timezone. Tech stack: Python, PostgreSQL, AWS.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start for standard engineering onboarding

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Onboarding with All Phases
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete onboarding plan including pre-arrival preparation, Day 1 agenda, Week 1 technical immersion, and 30/60/90 day milestones. New hire: Senior Frontend Engineer joining a hybrid team. Include React/TypeScript setup, team integration activities, and measurable success criteria for each milestone.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed plan for senior individual contributor

prompt_templates.title: Remote-Specific Onboarding
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an onboarding plan optimized for a fully remote new hire across 4 timezones. Include: virtual orientation schedule, async communication guidelines, home office setup checklist, virtual team bonding activities, and strategies to prevent isolation. Role: Product Designer.

prompt_templates.title: Leadership Onboarding Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an accelerated onboarding plan for a new Engineering Manager. Include: leadership team introductions, 1:1 schedule with direct reports, current project briefings, team health assessment framework, and first 90 day leadership goals. Team size: 12 engineers across 3 squads.

output_examples.input: Create Day 1 checklist for new remote software engineer
output: Morning: Manager welcome (30 min), HR paperwork (45 min), company culture overview (30 min), team standup observation (15 min)
Afternoon: Virtual lunch with buddy (60 min), laptop setup with IT (90 min), Slack and tools configuration (30 min), first Git commit ceremony (30 min)
End of day: Feedback survey (10 min), preparation for Day 2 agenda review

output_examples.input: What should a 30-day milestone include for a backend engineer
output: Complete all mandatory training modules and security certifications
Merge at least 3 pull requests including one bug fix deployed to production
Document one unclear process or system for future onboarding
Present key learnings to team in 10-minute lightning talk
Receive and discuss manager feedback with adjustment plan

best_practices: Customize onboarding plans based on role, seniority, and team context rather than using generic templates
Assign a dedicated onboarding buddy for the first 30 days with structured responsibilities and daily check-ins
Document everything during onboarding to create reusable artifacts for future new hires
Track measurable metrics like time to first commit and time to first production deploy
Prioritize human connection and team integration alongside technical setup and training
anti_patterns: Using one-size-fits-all onboarding without accounting for role differences or experience levels
Overwhelming new hires with too much information in the first week without prioritization
Focusing only on technical setup while neglecting team culture and relationship building
Leaving new hires without structured support or clear expectations after Day 1 orientation

faq.question: How long should a complete onboarding process take?
faq.answer: Effective onboarding spans 90 days with structured milestones at 30, 60, and 90 days. Day 1 focuses on welcome and setup, Week 1 on immersion, and the full 90 days enables progression from observation to independent contribution.

faq.question: What makes remote onboarding different from in-person onboarding?
faq.answer: Remote onboarding requires intentional virtual connection activities, async communication training, timezone-aware scheduling, and explicit documentation of norms that in-person teams absorb organically through physical presence.

faq.question: How do I measure onboarding success?
faq.answer: Track metrics like time to first commit, time to first production deploy, completion of training milestones, knowledge retention assessments, and new hire satisfaction scores through weekly pulse surveys.

faq.question: What is the buddy system and why is it important?
faq.answer: The buddy system pairs a new hire with an experienced team member for 30 days. Buddies provide daily check-ins, answer questions, guide through processes, and serve as a safe channel for concerns. This accelerates integration and reduces new hire anxiety.

faq.question: How should onboarding differ for senior or lead hires?
faq.answer: Senior hires need accelerated technical immersion, leadership introductions, early ownership opportunities, and involvement in architectural decisions. Compress the timeline while adding stakeholder mapping and strategic context sessions.

faq.question: What if a new hire is struggling during onboarding?
faq.answer: Increase buddy check-in frequency, schedule additional manager 1:1s, identify specific knowledge gaps, adjust the plan pace, and consider pairing with different team members. Early intervention in the first 30 days prevents longer-term issues.
